name: marathon
explanation: The check GETs Marathon REST API paths (`/v2/apps`, `/v2/deployments`, `/v2/queue`) under a single user-supplied `url`. The Marathon master listens on a well-known port (8080 for HTTP, 8443 for HTTPS), so a port-scan + path probe of `/v2/info` or `/v2/apps` could identify Marathon distinctively (the JSON shape is unambiguous). However, two complications push this into custom territory: (1) DC/OS deployments require ACS authentication via a separate `acs_url` plus username/password (or a richer `auth_token` reader/writer config) — discovery would have to detect ACS and follow the login dance; (2) self-hosted Marathon masters often run behind basic auth or a reverse proxy. The unauthenticated localhost case works for development and matches pattern E (auth-optional in spec, often gated in production). Without ACS/auth credentials the discovery layer can identify the endpoint but cannot complete the connection.
notes: Mesos/Marathon is largely sunsetted (DC/OS EOL); modern deployments are rare. Pattern E with an HTTP-path-probe edge: probe `/v2/info` on 8080 and 8443, fall through to credentials when 401.
